PatientProfile.Name: Robert Wells
PatientProfile.PhysicalTherapist: Bay Area PT Clinic, twice weekly

Medication.Name: carbidopa-levodopa (Sinemet)
Medication.Notes: Must be taken consistently; gaps cause significant symptom increase

Entry.MobilitySummary: Good. Walked driveway length without assistance. Gait change noted but manageable.
Entry.Mood: Good. Working morning crossword when arrived.
Entry.CaregiverBurden: Linda appears tired. Carrying daily medication timing and PT transportation alone.
Entry.Actions: Ordered shower chair for bathroom before leaving. Linda did not object.
Entry.Notes: Robert expressed missing purposeful morning routine from work years. Raised with Dr. Mitchell for structured hobby recommendation.

Entry.MobilitySummary: Improved. Physical therapy showing visible difference. Walked driveway length with Stephanie.
Entry.Mood: Reflective. Discussed walks at former employer during lunch breaks.
Entry.CaregiverBurden: Linda managing. Transportation to PT twice weekly confirmed.
Entry.Notes: Considering model building or hands-on hobby to provide morning structure and fine motor engagement.

Entry.MobilitySummary: Stable. Morning PT exercises completed before Stephanie arrived.
Entry.FallRisk: Low to medium - stair navigation noted as slower
Entry.CaregiverBurden: Linda managing. Agreed to grab bars after initial resistance.
Entry.Actions: Ordered grab bars for bathroom. Will install next visit.
Entry.Notes: Stair rail should be discussed at next neurologist appointment October 16, 2026.

Entry.MobilitySummary: Slightly reduced. Tremor higher than prior weeks, possibly due to poor sleep reported by Linda.
Entry.MedicationCompliance: One dose delay reported (lunch dose 45 minutes late two days ago)
Entry.CaregiverBurden: Linda visibly fatigued. Discussed respite care options briefly.
Entry.Actions: Installed grab bars in bathroom. Set phone alarm for Robert for noon dose as backup.
Entry.Notes: Flagged to Dr. Mitchell via patient portal message. Confirmed that dose timing is critical and should not slip more than 30 minutes.

Entry.MobilitySummary: Stable. Gait improved since February. PT twice weekly showing benefit.
Entry.Mood: Calm. Aware of wedding tomorrow. Wished Stephanie well.
Entry.CaregiverBurden: Linda stable. Kyle visiting from Seattle for the wedding weekend and providing support.
Entry.PhysicianAssessment: Disease at Stage 2. Tremor well-controlled on current medication. Fine motor tasks showing mild decline. Continue current protocol. Review stair rail at home. Fall risk is low-medium; recommend grab bars in additional bathroom (kitchen entry). Next appointment December 11, 2026.
Entry.Actions: Stephanie to coordinate stair rail installation with Kyle during Seattle visit. Linda to call clinic if any falls before December appointment.
Entry.Notes: Brought Robert and Linda to appointment. Robert drove to wedding the next day with Kyle as driver. Accessible seating arranged at venue front row.

Entry.MobilitySummary: Good. Back to baseline after travel fatigue from wedding weekend.
Entry.Mood: Happy. Enjoyed the wedding. Proud of Stephanie.
Entry.CaregiverBurden: Linda in good spirits. Kyle helped with stair rail installation during visit.
Entry.Actions: Stair rail installed. Confirm with Dr. Mitchell at December 11 appointment.
Entry.Notes: Next in-person visit planned for December 13, 2026 with groceries and holiday prep.

Appointment.Outcome: Routine quarterly check. Stage 2 confirmed. Current medication effective. No changes.
Stage 2 confirmed. Stable. Fine motor mild decline. Continue current protocol. Add grab bar in kitchen entry.

EmergencyPlan.Step: Call 911 for any fall with injury or loss of consciousness
